name: GaugeFlow Gate A7 joint atom-type set substrate v1
objective: Repair the graph-wise stoichiometry and sublattice correlation absent from A6's independent node posterior, while retaining its valid absorbing discrete flow for atomic species.

historical_evidence_is_immutable.instruction: A/A1/A2/A3/A4/A5/A6 reports, thresholds, splits, checkpoints and conclusions remain immutable. A7 is a new type-only set-decoder protocol.

gate_a6: The absorbing discrete path had exact analytic closure, zero terminal masks and a common-noise terminal discrete split of 0.75, proving that the discrete sampler itself works.

Endpoint-ID composition accuracy remained 0.75 and atom accuracy 0.46875. Decoded samples often contained the correct two elements but independent node draws changed the target 2:2 stoichiometry to 1:3; correct-count samples still exposed sublattice assignment errors.

A crystal atom-type state is a correlated finite set. Its graph-level composition must be generated from the condition rather than assumed as an external input or obtained by independently sampling nodes.

literature.citation: Gat et al., Discrete Flow Matching, 2024
literature.use: DFM permits arbitrary couplings and learned endpoint posteriors. A7 keeps the absorbing DFM path but models a graph-level count latent before constrained token transitions.

literature.citation: Miller et al., FlowMM: Generating Materials with Riemannian Flow Matching, 2024
literature.use: Crystal generation must respect permutation symmetry; A7 treats composition as a permutation-invariant graph quantity and only then assigns types to sites.

joint_type_set.chemical_vocabulary: all 119 chemical elements; the DFM mask is internal and cannot occur in a composition count
joint_type_set.composition_latent: for each element k, predict count n_k in [0,N] from the graph condition and fully masked type state; decode MAP/sample under the exact sum_k n_k=N constraint
joint_type_set.node_transition: retain the A6 absorbing DFM posterior. At every reveal event, jointly match nodes to the still-available element-count slots using posterior log probabilities plus fixed common Gumbels.

endpoint_id_type_substrate.condition: two-class endpoint ID only; no tensor, orbit, oracle, target CIF, target lattice, target composition or target symmetry is passed as a condition
endpoint_id_type_substrate.note: For this type-only audit true coordinates and lattice are held fixed exactly as in A4-A6, isolating type-set generation.

decision_rules.pass: The type set substrate is qualified only. Propose a separate versioned geometry-only repair; do not start joint geometry or tensor conditioning.
decision_rules.composition_pass_site_fail: Do not relax composition constraints. Audit site-assignment identifiability under the periodic equivariant backbone before any tensor work.
decision_rules.composition_fail: Audit graph-level condition access and count-posterior capacity. Do not return to independent per-node type sampling or target-specific vocabularies.
